ci-cd/quality-gate-action.title: Quality Gate Action
ci-cd/quality-gate-action.description: The Foxhound quality gate GitHub Actions composite action enforces evaluation score thresholds on every pull request. When scores fall below your configured threshold, the action fails the check and posts a summary comment to the PR.

evaluation-cookbook/ci-quality-gates.title: CI Quality Gates
evaluation-cookbook/ci-quality-gates.description: Quality gates enforce evaluation score thresholds on every pull request. When scores drop below your configured threshold, the gate fails the check and posts a detailed comment to the PR. This prevents regressions from merging undetected.

evaluation-cookbook/dataset-curation.title: Dataset Curation
evaluation-cookbook/dataset-curation.description: A good evaluation dataset is a curated sample of traces that represents the range of behaviors you care about — successful runs, edge cases, and known failure modes. Foxhound provides two curation workflows: adding individual traces manually, and bulk-curating by score thresholds.

evaluation-cookbook/index.title: Evaluation Cookbook
evaluation-cookbook/index.description: Foxhound provides a layered evaluation system that lets you move from quick manual feedback all the way to automated regression prevention in CI. This cookbook covers each layer with practical how-to guides.

evaluation-cookbook/llm-as-a-judge.title: LLM-as-a-Judge
evaluation-cookbook/llm-as-a-judge.description: LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators let you score traces automatically using a language model as the judge. Foxhound runs evaluators asynchronously — you trigger a run against a set of trace IDs and poll for results.

evaluation-cookbook/manual-scoring.title: Manual Scoring
evaluation-cookbook/manual-scoring.description: Manual scoring is the fastest way to attach quality signal to a trace. You score directly from your IDE using two MCP tools: foxhoundscoretrace (write a score) and foxhoundgettrace_scores (read all scores on a trace).

getting-started/first-trace.title: Your First Trace
getting-started/first-trace.description: After running the Quickstart, your trace is visible in the Foxhound UI. This guide walks you through what you'll see.

getting-started/installation.title: Installation
getting-started/installation.description: Foxhound provides SDKs for Python and TypeScript, plus an MCP server for IDE integration.

getting-started/quickstart.title: Quickstart
getting-started/quickstart.description: Send your first trace to Foxhound in under five minutes.

index.title: Foxhound Documentation
index.description: Compliance-grade observability for AI agent fleets.

integrations/bedrock-agentcore.title: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
integrations/bedrock-agentcore.description: Foxhound integrates with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore via the OpenTelemetry bridge and a convenience helper that wires everything up in one call.

integrations/crewai.title: CrewAI
integrations/crewai.description: Foxhound integrates with CrewAI via FoxCrewTracer, which hooks into step and task callbacks to capture agent activity.

integrations/google-adk.title: Google ADK
integrations/google-adk.description: Foxhound integrates with Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) via the OpenTelemetry bridge. Google ADK emits OTel spans when tracing is enabled on AdkApp.

integrations/langgraph.title: LangGraph
integrations/langgraph.description: Foxhound integrates with LangGraph and LangChain via a callback handler that captures every step — LLM calls, tool invocations, and chain transitions.

integrations/mastra.title: Mastra
integrations/mastra.description: Foxhound integrates with Mastra via the OpenTelemetry span processor bridge. Mastra emits OTel GenAI semantic convention spans natively, so no framework-specific hooks are needed.

integrations/opentelemetry-bridge.title: OpenTelemetry Bridge
integrations/opentelemetry-bridge.description: The Foxhound OTel bridge lets any framework that emits OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic convention spans send traces to Foxhound — one import, zero framework code changes.

integrations/pydantic-ai.title: Pydantic AI
integrations/pydantic-ai.description: Foxhound integrates with Pydantic AI via the OpenTelemetry span processor bridge. Pydantic AI emits OTel GenAI spans when instrument=True is set on the agent.

mcp-server/setup.title: MCP Server Setup
mcp-server/setup.description: The Foxhound MCP server exposes your traces and observability data to Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and any other MCP-connected IDE, letting your AI assistant query traces, surface anomalies, explain failures, and score runs — all without leaving your editor.

mcp-server/tool-reference.title: MCP Tool Reference
mcp-server/tool-reference.description: The Foxhound MCP server exposes 30 tools, grouped by capability.

sdk/python.title: Python SDK
sdk/python.description: Compliance-grade observability for AI agent fleets — trace, replay, and audit every agent decision.

sdk/typescript.title: TypeScript SDK
sdk/typescript.description: Compliance-grade observability for AI agent fleets — trace, replay, and audit every agent decision.
